query: Which top 3 entities had the most number of filings in FERC's rule making proceedings on large load interconnection in December 2025?
final_answer:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the FERC eLibrary data for December 2025, here are the results:

## Top 3 Entities by Number of Filings in FERC Rulemaking Proceedings on Large Load Interconnection (December 2025)

The key rulemaking proceeding is **Docket RM26-4** — the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANOPR) on *"Ensuring the Timely and Orderly Interconnection of Large Loads to the Interstate Transmission System,"* initiated by FERC on October 27, 2025. December 2025 was the deadline for reply comments (due December 5), generating a flood of filings. Related proceedings (EL26-30, EL25-49, AD24-11, EL25-20) also addressed large-load interconnection issues in PJM.

### #1: **PJM Interconnection, L.L.C. — 4 filings**

| Date | Docket | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 12/05 | EL25-49/AD24-11/EL25-20 | Answer to Constellation's Motion for Expedited Action |
| 12/11 | RM26-4 | Reply Comments on the Large Load ANOPR |
| 12/15 | EL26-30 | Answer to the IMM Complaint on large loads |
| 12/29 | RM26-4/EL25-49/EL25-20/EL26-30/AD24-11 | Request to update service lists across all related dockets |

**Source:** FERC eLibrary searches ([RM26-4 dockets](https://elibrary.ferc.gov), [EL26-30 dockets](https://elibrary.ferc.gov))

### #2: **Constellation Energy Generation, LLC — 2 filings**

| Date | Docket | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 12/05 | RM26-4 | Supporting Reply Comments on the Large Load ANOPR |
| 12/05 | EL25-49/AD24-11/EL25-20 | Motion to File Supplemental Comments |

**Source:** FERC eLibrary ([RM26-4 results](https://elibrary.ferc.gov), [EL25-49 results](https://elibrary.ferc.gov))

### #3: **Eight entities tied at 2 filings each**

| Entity | Filings |
|--------|---------|
| **Monitoring Analytics, LLC** (IMM for PJM) | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + EL26-30 answer to answers (12/19) |
| **Virginia State Corporation Commission** | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + EL25-49 answer (12/15) |
| **FirstEnergy Service Company** | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + EL26-30 answer (12/15) |
| **Old Dominion Electric Cooperative** | EL26-30 comments (12/15) + EL25-49 answer (12/8) |
| **Exelon Corporation** | EL25-49 indicated TOs answer (12/8) + standalone Exelon answer (12/8) |
| **Switch, Ltd.** | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + doc-less motion to intervene (12/5) |
| **Electric Power Supply Association** | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + EL26-30 joint protest with P3 (12/15) |
| **Southern Environmental Law Center** | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + also represented multiple co-filers |

**Source:** FERC eLibrary ([RM26-4 docket page](https://elibrary.ferc.gov/eLibrary/search), multiple search passes spanning all large-load interconnection dockets)

---

### Summary

PJM Interconnection, as the grid operator at the center of the large-load interconnection debate, was far and away the most active filer with **4 submissions** across the rulemaking (RM26-4) and related complaint/enforcement proceedings. Constellation Energy Generation was the most active large-load developer/interested party with 2 filings. The remaining entities all filed twice across the interconnected proceedings, reflecting the broad stakeholder interest — from state commissions and consumer advocates to data center developers, environmental groups, and competitive power suppliers.

steps.content:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the FERC eLibrary data for December 2025, here are the results:

## Top 3 Entities by Number of Filings in FERC Rulemaking Proceedings on Large Load Interconnection (December 2025)

The key rulemaking proceeding is **Docket RM26-4** — the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANOPR) on *"Ensuring the Timely and Orderly Interconnection of Large Loads to the Interstate Transmission System,"* initiated by FERC on October 27, 2025. December 2025 was the deadline for reply comments (due December 5), generating a flood of filings. Related proceedings (EL26-30, EL25-49, AD24-11, EL25-20) also addressed large-load interconnection issues in PJM.

### #1: **PJM Interconnection, L.L.C. — 4 filings**

| Date | Docket | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 12/05 | EL25-49/AD24-11/EL25-20 | Answer to Constellation's Motion for Expedited Action |
| 12/11 | RM26-4 | Reply Comments on the Large Load ANOPR |
| 12/15 | EL26-30 | Answer to the IMM Complaint on large loads |
| 12/29 | RM26-4/EL25-49/EL25-20/EL26-30/AD24-11 | Request to update service lists across all related dockets |

**Source:** FERC eLibrary searches ([RM26-4 dockets](https://elibrary.ferc.gov), [EL26-30 dockets](https://elibrary.ferc.gov))

### #2: **Constellation Energy Generation, LLC — 2 filings**

| Date | Docket | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 12/05 | RM26-4 | Supporting Reply Comments on the Large Load ANOPR |
| 12/05 | EL25-49/AD24-11/EL25-20 | Motion to File Supplemental Comments |

**Source:** FERC eLibrary ([RM26-4 results](https://elibrary.ferc.gov), [EL25-49 results](https://elibrary.ferc.gov))

### #3: **Eight entities tied at 2 filings each**

| Entity | Filings |
|--------|---------|
| **Monitoring Analytics, LLC** (IMM for PJM) | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + EL26-30 answer to answers (12/19) |
| **Virginia State Corporation Commission** | RM26-4 reply comments (12/5) + EL25-49 answer (12/15) |
